metadata.title: Why the Restriction Point Is a One-Way Gate, Not a Dial
metadata.purpose: vox-explainer slate cut of Cancer Biology candidate 23. The Rb/E2F bistable switch — why growth-factor withdrawal stops a pre-restriction cell but has no effect on a post-restriction cell.
metadata.clock: narration (ElevenLabs) — durations below are word-count estimates until GATE 0 audio lock
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL = committed cell / positive feedback / S-phase entry / post-restriction; CRIMSON = growth-factor withdrawal / threshold / one-way gate / pre-restriction return to G0; never swap mid-film
metadata.note: EXCLUSIONS (honored absolutely): no CDK4/6 inhibitor therapy, no Rb/E2F cancer mutations, no p16/CDKN2A, no p53/p21 checkpoint cascade, no G2/M or spindle checkpoint, no cancer drug discussion. Manim move: transform (ball on hill energy landscape). Still lanes: geo (bistable energy landscape diagram), geo (cyclin D and cyclin E level timelines with restriction point marked). Prerequisites assumed: cyclins/CDKs basics, transcription factor concept.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Pragmatist register (voices/pragmatist/VOICE.md + HAI.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the HAI outro from AUTHOR.MD :: Humanitarians AI
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: Two cells. Same genome, same growth medium, same signal arriving now. Remove the signal in thirty minutes: one cell stops, one does not. Same withdrawal, opposite outcomes.

card.copy: Why the Restriction Point Is a One-Way Gate, Not a Dial

beats.new_visual_element: [TITLE] title card with CANCER BIOLOGY eyebrow

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [TITLE] title card with CANCER BIOLOGY eyebrow

beats.narration_text: Cell A: early G1. Growth signals arriving, cyclin D accumulating, Rb hypophosphorylated, E2F captured. Division not yet committed.

production_viz.label: Cell A: pre-restriction — cyclin D rising, Rb unphosphorylated, E2F captured
production_viz.mechanic: Left side: a CRIMSON chip labeled 'CELL A' above a simple state diagram. Rb block shown in CRIMSON (gate closed). Cyclin D level bar: low-to-medium, rising. E2F icon locked behind Rb. Arrow from 'Growth signals' pointing into cyclin D bar.

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] Cell A state: pre-restriction — E2F locked

beats.narration_text: Cell B: thirty minutes later in the same cycle. Cyclin E transcribed, CDK2 phosphorylating Rb, E2F driving S-phase genes. Committed.

production_viz.label: Cell B: post-restriction — cyclin E on, CDK2 active, Rb hyperphosphorylated, E2F free
production_viz.mechanic: Right side: TEAL chip labeled 'CELL B'. Rb block shown TEAL (gate open, hyperphosphorylated). Cyclin E bar: high, glowing TEAL. E2F icon freed, arrow pointing toward S-phase genes. A positive-feedback loop arrow visible.

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] Cell B state: post-restriction — E2F free, S-phase on

beats.narration_text: Growth factor withdrawal should stop division — that is the signal's function. Same withdrawal: Cell A stops completely, Cell B is unaffected. Why does position relative to one threshold determine everything?

card.copy: Growth factor withdrawal should stop division.
card.dek: Same signal removed. One cell stops. One cell doesn't. Why does timing relative to the restriction point determine everything?

beats.new_visual_element: [CARD] THE QUESTION — gap formula on screen

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [CARD] THE QUESTION — gap formula on screen

beats.narration_text: The linear model: commitment scales with signal strength. More growth factor, higher division probability. Remove signal, probability drops. All cells follow the same equation. This model predicts both cells stop — it is wrong.

production_viz.label: naive model: commitment as a continuous dial
production_viz.mechanic: A horizontal dial labeled 'Growth Signal' from 0 to HIGH. An arrow labeled 'Commitment' rises proportionally. Label above: 'More signal -> more division'. Shown in CRIMSON to flag the naive wrong model.

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] naive dial model — commitment proportional to signal

beats.narration_text: If commitment were a dial, removing the signal would reverse the decision. A dial turns back. Cell B's decision does not reverse after signal removal. The dial model fails here — the system is not reversible.

production_viz.label: dial model fails — withdrawal should reverse Cell B but doesn't
production_viz.mechanic: Same dial from B05. Growth signal arrow drops to zero. For Cell A: commitment bar drops (correct prediction). For Cell B: commitment bar stays high, TEAL, does not drop. A CRIMSON X appears over the dial model's prediction for Cell B.

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] dial prediction fails for Cell B

beats.narration_text: The actual mechanism: Rb holds E2F transcription factors bound at S-phase gene promoters. Cyclin D and CDK4/6 begin phosphorylating Rb. Partial phosphorylation releases some E2F, which transcribes cyclin E.

props.headline: Here is the actual switch. Rb, the retinoblastoma protein, holds E2F…

beats.scene_description: Schematic diagram on newsprint: Rb as a locked gate, E2F held behind it, growth-factor signal entering cyclin D, cyclin D feeding CDK4/6, CDK4/6 partially phosphorylating Rb, partial opening, E2F begins to escape toward cyclin E gene, editorial diagram style, flat print, no shadows
beats.image_prompt: B07, schematic cell-biology circuit diagram on aged newsprint, Rb protein as a padlocked gate with E2F trapped behind it, growth-factor signal arrow pointing into a cyclin D bar, cyclin D activating CDK4/6 complex, CDK4/6 beginning to phosphorylate Rb with one phosphate dot labeled, partial gate opening, E2F escaping to point at cyclin E gene box, flat editorial illustration, desaturated, no drop shadows, news-diagram style

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [STILL] Rb/E2F circuit diagram — partial phosphorylation

beats.narration_text: Cyclin E pairs with CDK2. CDK2 further phosphorylates Rb — a positive feedback loop. More E2F released, more cyclin E transcribed, more CDK2 activity, more Rb phosphorylated. The loop is self-sustaining.

production_viz.label: positive feedback loop: cyclin E -> CDK2 -> Rb phosphorylation -> E2F -> cyclin E
production_viz.mechanic: A circular arrow loop in TEAL. Nodes: cyclin E -> CDK2 -> Rb(hyperP) -> E2F free -> cyclin E. Each node lights up TEAL as the loop is traced. A serif label 'positive feedback' with hairline underline sits beside the loop.

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] positive feedback loop — TEAL cyclin E/CDK2/Rb/E2F circuit

beats.narration_text: This is bistability. Below the threshold: Rb stays bound, E2F stays silent. Above the threshold: positive feedback drives the system to full E2F release. Two stable states, sharp transition — a switch, not a gradient.

props.headline: This is bistability. Below threshold, Rb stays bound and E2F stays…

beats.scene_description: Energy landscape diagram on newsprint: a ball-on-a-hill schematic with two wells. Left well labeled 'G0 / G1 (Rb bound)' in CRIMSON. Hill summit labeled 'Restriction Point threshold'. Right well labeled 'S phase committed (Rb hyperP)' in TEAL. The ball sits in the left well. The hill is asymmetric — left well shallow, right well deep. Flat editorial illustration, aged newsprint ground, no digital glows.
beats.image_prompt: B09, bistable energy landscape diagram on aged newsprint, x-axis labeled 'Rb phosphorylation state', y-axis labeled 'Energy', two potential wells: left shallow well labeled G0 / G1 in dark red, right deep well labeled S phase committed in dark teal, hill summit labeled Restriction Point, a small circle ball sitting in the left shallow well, black ink line, flat editorial diagram, no gradients, no shadows, newsprint-collage style
beats.new_visual_element: [STILL] bistable energy landscape — two wells, restriction point summit

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [STILL] bistable energy landscape — two wells, restriction point summit

beats.narration_text: Growth signals push the system toward the threshold. For Cell A — pre-restriction — when signal stops, cyclin D falls within minutes (it is intrinsically unstable). Rb returns to hypophosphorylated state. E2F is recaptured. Cell exits to G0.

production_viz.label: Cell A: ball pushed toward summit, signal stops, ball rolls back left
production_viz.mechanic: Energy landscape hill. CRIMSON ball starts in left well, pushed rightward (growth signal arrow). Signal arrow disappears. Ball rolls back down into left well. Label below left well: 'G0 — cyclin D falls, Rb hypophosphorylated, E2F captured'.

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] Cell A ball rolls back to G0 — cyclin D falls

beats.narration_text: For Cell B — post-restriction — the system has crossed the threshold into the deep right well. Positive feedback is running. Cyclin E is now E2F-driven, not growth-factor-driven. Removing growth factors has no effect on cyclin E. The commitment is complete; the initiating signal is no longer needed.

production_viz.label: Cell B: ball already in right well — signal removal has no effect
production_viz.mechanic: Same energy landscape. TEAL ball starts in right deep well. Growth signal arrow disappears. Ball stays in right well — does not move. Label: 'S phase committed — cyclin E is E2F-driven, not growth-factor-driven'. Feedback loop arrow shown active in TEAL.

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] Cell B ball stays in S-phase well — irreversible

beats.narration_text: Two cells, thirty minutes apart. Growth factors drop to zero simultaneously. Cell A: cyclin D half-life is fifteen minutes — eliminated in thirty minutes. Rb becomes hypophosphorylated. E2F recaptured. Cell exits to G0. Cell B: cyclin E is already being transcribed by E2F. CDK2 is already phosphorylating Rb. Removing growth signals does not reduce cyclin E — it is E2F-driven now. The cell enters S phase on schedule. Same withdrawal event. Opposite outcomes. Position relative to one threshold is the only variable that differs.

production_viz.label: two-cell comparison: same withdrawal, opposite fates
production_viz.mechanic: Two columns side by side. Left column: CRIMSON chip 'CELL A — pre-restriction'. Timeline shows: t=0 growth factors drop, cyclin D level bar falls over 30 min (illustrative), Rb hypophosphorylated icon, E2F captured icon, final label 'G0'. Right column: TEAL chip 'CELL B — post-restriction'. Same t=0 withdrawal. Cyclin E bar stays high (E2F-driven, not GF-driven). Rb hyperP icon. Final label 'S phase'. Dashed vertical line at t=0 labeled 'GF withdrawal'.

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] two-cell comparison — same withdrawal, G0 vs S phase

beats.narration_text: Growth factor withdrawal stops division only before the threshold. After it, the Rb-E2F positive feedback loop sustains itself without the signal that started it. The restriction point is a one-way gate because the switch is bistable — not a dial.

card.copy: The restriction point is a one-way gate because the Rb/E2F switch is bistable.

beats.new_visual_element: [CARD] endcard — question restated then answered, CANCER BIOLOGY kicker

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [CARD] endcard — question restated then answered, CANCER BIOLOGY kicker
